key_points:
### Key Compliance Points — Credit Facilities (Amendment) Directions, 2026
*(RBI/2026-27/113, dated 2026-06-10; effective 2026-10-01)*

Opens up AIFI **lending to Infrastructure Investment Trusts (InvITs)**, including acquisition finance (RBI/2026-27/113, 2026-06-10).

- **Deletion:** In Chapter VI, **paragraphs 123–126 are deleted**.
- **New 126A–126B — Lending to InvITs (general):**
  - AIFIs may lend to InvITs **registered with and regulated by SEBI**; Board-approved policy required (appraisal, DSCR benchmarks, exposure limits, monitoring covenants, valuation methodology).
  - Lend only to a **listed** InvIT, with **≥80%** of asset value in **completed, revenue-generating** infrastructure projects generating positive operating cash flows for **≥1 year**.
  - Monitor end-use; do not fund SPVs in financial difficulty; refinancing only for completed projects at COD; **no bullet/ballooning** repayment structures (except bond/debenture/CP investment exposures).
- **New 126C — Acquisition finance to InvITs:**
  - Permitted to acquire **'control'** (as per Companies Act s.2(27)) of a **non-financial infrastructure** target, as a strategic (synergy-driven) investment; acquiring company must have **standalone & consolidated net worth ≥ ₹500 crore**.
  - Total lender financing **≤ 75% of the acquisition value** (independent valuation per SEBI SAST norms); control within **12 months** of first disbursal; acquirer's own claims on the target rank **subordinate** to the AIFI; not for targets with financial-entity subsidiaries/JVs; aggregate acquisition-finance exposure within the **capital-market exposure limit** (Concentration Risk Directions para 52).
- **126D — Leverage ceiling:** InvIT's overall leverage within **SEBI's prescribed ceiling** (or a lower Board limit).
- **126E — Security:** financing **fully secured** (first/pari-passu charge on immovable property, assignment of cash flows/receivables, pledge of SPV equity), with escrow ring-fencing and lender step-in/termination protections.
- **Commencement:** **October 1, 2026** (or earlier if fully adopted); non-conforming existing InvIT loans may run off till maturity but cannot be renewed/enhanced unless compliant.

_Verify with the compliance team before acting._
what_changed:
### What Changed
*(Amendment: RBI/2026-27/113, dated 2026-06-10 · effective 2026-10-01)*

| Provision | Change type | Effect |
|---|---|---|
| **Chapter VI, paras 123–126** | **Deleted** | Removed. |
| **Paras 126A–126B** | **Inserted** | Permits lending to SEBI-registered, listed InvITs (≥80% completed revenue-generating assets), with Board policy, end-use monitoring and no bullet/balloon repayment. |
| **Para 126C** | **Inserted** | Acquisition finance to InvITs for 'control' of non-financial infra targets: ≥₹500cr net worth, ≤75% of acquisition value, 12-month control window, subordinated acquirer claims, capital-market exposure limit. |
| **Para 126D** | **Inserted** | InvIT leverage within SEBI ceiling / lower Board limit. |
| **Para 126E** | **Inserted** | Full security + escrow ring-fencing + lender protections. |

- **Effective date:** October 1, 2026 (existing non-conforming loans may run off till maturity).

_Verify with the compliance team before acting._
implications:
**Why it matters for NaBFID:** This directly expands NaBFID's core mandate — infrastructure financing — by allowing lending to InvITs and even financing the acquisition of control over infrastructure companies, subject to strong prudential guardrails (listing, ≥80% completed assets, ≤75% acquisition-value cap, full security).

*Illustrative example:* NaBFID could extend acquisition finance to an infra-focused acquiring InvIT (net worth ≥ ₹500 crore) to acquire control of an operating toll-road company — lending up to 75% of the independently-valued acquisition price, secured by the shares acquired, with the acquirer's own claims subordinated to NaBFID.

_Verify with the compliance team before acting._
